does_not: execute any command token from the conflicting activation

runtime_witnesses.before: wire/right recipient with wire-r-init and proc-l-init on direct input
runtime_witnesses.summary: A fixed recipient with two init-family command-message tokens rejects and clears the direct input instead of choosing one command.

runtime_witnesses.before: proc/left recipient with wire-r-init and proc-l-init on upstream input
runtime_witnesses.summary: A fixed recipient that pulls two upstream command-message tokens rejects the conflict and clears the pulled upstream source.

runtime_witnesses.before: stem recipient with wire-r-init and write-buf-one on direct input
runtime_witnesses.summary: A stem recipient with multiple command-message tokens rejects the conflict while preserving command control and buffer state.

claim_dependency.summary: The existing rejection predicate already covers conflicting command-message input; ADR-0059 adds an all-init conflict example so the selected policy is visible in the claim manifest.

implemented_traces.summary: AS now has a schematic-linked trace for a fixed recipient rejecting two simultaneous init-family command-message tokens.

implemented_svgs.summary: AS now has a rendered SVG view checked exactly against the multi-command recipient rejection trace.

implemented_evidence_bundles.summary: AS now has an integrated evidence bundle validating one multi-command rejection boundary across claim, proof, trace, render, and source-status layers.

as_boundary: AS accepts exactly one init-family recipient command-message input. Two or more command-message tokens in the same recipient activation are rejected and cleared; no priority or sequencing rule is inferred.
